module_map.responsibility: Orchestrator of the file listing pipeline. Coordinates fetch, sort, and display stages. Manages lifecycle from Flags through output.
Metadata extraction and representation. Collects filesystem attributes (permissions, size, owner, date, links, git status) and builds Meta struct. Houses per-file semantic information.
Output rendering. Transforms Meta + Flags + Colors + Icons into formatted grid or tree output. Handles column layout, alignment, coloring, and terminal width constraints.
CLI and config parsing, feature flags, and command-line option collection. Covers blocks, color, layout, size, sorting, icons, permissions, dates, recursion, etc.
Sorting logic for Meta entries. Provides sorters by name, size, date, extension, version, git status, and directory-first grouping. Recursively sorts nested content.
Theme and styling backend. Provides color schemes and icon themes for display output.
Color resolution and application. Wraps lscolors and theme backends to colorize elements (permissions, size, date, file types, etc.).
Icon resolution and retrieval by filename, extension, or file type. Applies theme-specific icon substitution.
Git status tracking and caching. Provides GitCache and GitStatus for display and sorting.
YAML configuration loading and merging with CLI flags. Resolves flag precedence (CLI > config > defaults).

types_produced: Meta {name, path, permissions_or_attributes, date, owner, file_type, size, symlink, indicator, inode, links, content, access_control, git_status}

cross_module_hubs.reason: Flags is a central configuration hub parsed from CLI and config file. Nearly every other module depends on Flags for behavior control: core, display, sort, meta, icon, color, theme, config_file all read flags to customize behavior. Low outbound deps—mostly reads from config_file and delegates to submodules.

Meta is the core data structure representing file entries. core.rs fetches Metas, sort.rs sorts Metas, display.rs renders Metas. meta/ submodules interconnect: name, size, date, owner, etc. High outbound to git, icon, flags for semantic computation. Traversal and total_size calculation are recursive.

Display is the final rendering stage depending on Meta, Flags, Colors, Icons, GitTheme, and theme. Accepts formatted Metas and produces human-readable grid/tree output. High outbound deps for styling and layout logic. Called once per run from core.display().

Color resolves all styling via Elem categories (Permission, Size, Date, FileType, etc.). Depends on theme/color and lscolors for actual color data. display.rs and git_file_status.rs depend on color.rs for colorization.
Icon looks up theme-based icons by Name and FileType. Called from display during render. Depends on theme/icon and meta/name for matching logic.

new_types: Checksum: struct wrapping SHA-256 hash string, with display and color methods (similar to Size, Date, Owner pattern)
Block::Checksum: new enum variant in flags/blocks.rs to allow --blocks checksum (or --checksum flag)
ChecksumOption: flag enum to control checksum computation (off, lazy on-demand, or eager)
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Follow the Size and INode pattern: (1) Define Checksum struct in meta/checksum.rs wrapping the hash value. (2) Add Option<Checksum> field to Meta struct in meta/mod.rs. (3) Populate it in Meta::from_path() with conditional SHA-256 computation based on flags. (4) Add Block::Checksum to blocks.rs and Block::get_header() to return 'CHECKSUM'. (5) In display.rs get_output(), add a case for Block::Checksum to render the hash. (6) In theme/color.rs add Checksum color mapping (likely to Elem::Size or a new category).
change_proposal.integration_point: Block/display: Add Block::Checksum as a new column block option via --blocks checksum or a dedicated --checksum flag. The core.rs pipeline remains unchanged—meta collection populates Checksum, display renders it, no changes to sort or color logic needed unless sorting by checksum is desired.
risks: Performance: SHA-256 computation on every file can be expensive for large directories. Mitigation: Make it opt-in (off by default) and lazy (only compute if block is requested).
File I/O contention: Reading entire file for hash may thrash disk. Mitigation: Parallelize with rayon or stream hashing for large files.
Breaking change: Adding a new block changes the default columns if placed early in the block list. Mitigation: Insert at the end of the default block list or require explicit opt-in.
Caching complexity: Hash caches (mtime-keyed) would require new cache structure and invalidation logic. Defer to Wave 4 (caching/performance).
No cross-platform testing: Windows file hashing may behave differently (symlinks, virtual files). Add platform-specific tests.

tool_usage.params: mode=overview, path=/tmp/lsd-benchmark-target/src, max_depth=2 — directory tree with LOC and function counts
mode=file_details, path=/tmp/lsd-benchmark-target/src/meta/mod.rs — Meta struct, from_path, recurse_into functions
mode=file_details, path=/tmp/lsd-benchmark-target/src/core.rs — Core orchestration (fetch, sort, display pipeline)
mode=file_details, path=/tmp/lsd-benchmark-target/src/display.rs — grid, tree, get_output, padding logic
mode=file_details, path=/tmp/lsd-benchmark-target/src/flags.rs — Flags struct and Configurable trait
mode=file_details, path=/tmp/lsd-benchmark-target/src/flags/blocks.rs — Block enum, Block::get_header, Blocks collection
mode=file_details, path=/tmp/lsd-benchmark-target/src/color.rs — Colors struct, Elem enum, colorize logic
